title: cryptocoryne lingua
intro: Cryptocoryne lingua, also known as the tongue plant, is a type of aquatic plant belonging to the family Arum. It is native to Sri Lanka and is widely cultivated for its medicinal and ornamental value. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and digestive issues, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and decoctions, which are believed to offer health benefits such as reducing inflammation, improving respiratory function, and supporting immune system.
